title: freirea floridana
intro: Freirea floridana, commonly known as the West Indian bitterwood or bitterwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its reported health benefits, including its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation. The therapeutic actions of Freirea floridana are attributed to its bioactive constituents, which include alkaloids and flavonoids that exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and vasodilatory properties. Research on the plant's bioactive constituents is ongoing, but existing studies suggest that it may possess hepatoprotective, anti-diabetic, and anti-cancer properties.
side_effects: Freirea floridana can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Ingesting the plant's toxic compounds can lead to stomach pain, nausea, and vomiting. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can cause skin irritation and blistering. Furthermore, Freirea floridana can also cause cardiovascular issues, including rapid heartbeat and decreased blood pressure, if used in large quantities or for extended periods.
